UUID_purpose: Ambarella SoC base UUID placeholder/sentinel. Ambarella is the SoC vendor for many action cameras and dashcams (GoPro Hero up to 5, DJI, Garmin VIRB, SJCAM, Hawk Eye Firefly). All-zero high bytes here likely represent an unprovisioned/null vendor service ID.

evidence_array.description: Bytes `00 00 00 00 61 6d 62 61 5f 69 64 5f 62 61 73 65` literally spell ASCII '....amba_id_base'. Ambarella's published SDK uses 'amba_' prefix throughout.

Davidgyoung's BackgroundAdvertiser docs explicitly note: 'this is the Apple watch's 128-bit service UUID; avoid this bit position if you don't want your service confused with an Apple Watch'.

evidence_array.description: Apple's `ATTUUIDs.plist` (shipped with macOS PacketLogger.app's PacketDecoder.framework) maps `9AA4730F-B25C-4CC3-B821-C931559FC196` to the literal string 'Apple Accessory Reconnect Service'. Cross-confirmed in Apple iOS firmware (nanoregistryd's EPCheckBluetoothForIRK, EPAdvertiserManager, EPDiscovererManager, EPCharacteristicWriter, EPSagaTransactionPairing) and in Apple PairingProximity.framework / Sharing.framework / CoreBluetooth.framework binary strings.

evidence_array.description: ASCII decode of bytes = literal text '00000000DECAFADE' — the human-readable hex form of Apple iAP2's iPhone-side service UUID. Almost certainly a buggy device that stored literal text instead of binary UUID.

evidence_array.description: The canonical (non-swapped) form d31fbf50-5d57-2797-a240-41cd484388ec is documented in bluez issue #963 as 'CarPlay vehicle-side'; ec884348-cd41-40a2-9727-575d50bf1fd3 is the byte-reverse-of-fields-1-2-3 form of that same UUID.

evidence_array.description: Canonical ANCS UUID is 7905F431-B5CE-4E99-A40F-4B1E122D00D0; this is the single-nibble variant (4B1E120D vs 4B1E122D).

evidence_array.description: Fitbit's official bitgatt README uses ABCDEFGH-6E7D-4601-BDA2-BFFAA68956BA as the scan-filter placeholder, confirming the family-base structure. Schema documentation also explicitly cites this exact family as the canonical regex example.

UUID_purpose: Family-base regex for Fitbit's Project Golden Gate Gattlink Service (legacy custom-UUID variant; newer trackers use SIG 16-bit alias 0xFD62 instead). abbaff00 = service; abbaff01 = ReceiveCharacteristic; abbaff02 = TransmitCharacteristic. Observed on Charge 4, Charge 5, Inspire 3, Ionic, Luxe, Sense, Sense 2, Versa, Versa 2, Versa 3, Versa 4, Versa Lite.

evidence_array.description: Fitbit's open-source golden-gate framework defines the family with abbaff00 service + abbaff01/02 characteristics.

UUID_purpose: Fitbit Project Golden Gate's link-layer protocol service for reliable streaming of arbitrary data over BLE GATT; the legacy custom-UUID variant hosted on older trackers before Fitbit registered the SIG 16-bit alias 0xFD62 for the newer FitbitGattlinkService. Observed on Charge 4, Charge 5, Inspire 3, Ionic, Luxe, Sense, Sense 2, Versa, Versa 2, Versa 3, Versa 4, Versa Lite.

evidence_array.description: Fitbit's open-source golden-gate framework explicitly defines `class GattlinkService` with `uuid = UUID.fromString('ABBAFF00-E56A-484C-B832-8B17CF6CBFE8')`; doc comment notes that newer trackers use FitbitGattlinkService (UUID 0000FD62) instead. ReceiveCharacteristic.kt uses ABBAFF01 in the same family.

UUID_purpose: Fitbit's BLE advertising/discovery service for legacy 'Airlink-protocol' trackers (Ace 2, Ace 3, Alta, Alta HR, Blaze, Charge 2, Charge 3, Charge HR, Flex 2, Inspire, Inspire 2, Inspire HR, Ionic, Versa, Versa Lite, Zip); the first 32 bits XXXXXXXX-6e7d-4601-bda2-bffaa68956ba encode the product-type code so the Fitbit companion app can identify the model during BLE scanning.

evidence_array.description: Fitbit's bitgatt README uses `ABCDEFGH-6E7D-4601-BDA2-BFFAA68956BA` as the scan-filter placeholder, confirming the XXXXXXXX-6E7D-4601-BDA2-BFFAA68956BA structure where XXXXXXXX is the product-type code. reelyactive's BLE identifier reference also documents the pattern.

UUID_purpose: Onewheel-related BLE service sharing the same UUIDv1 node MAC 0800200c9a66 as the main Onewheel service (e659f300-ea98-11e3-ac10-0800200c9a66). Note: 0800200c9a66 is the RFC 4122 sample MAC, used by many UUIDv1 generators; the timestamp era (Aug 2013) and family suggest Onewheel.

evidence_array.description: UUIDv1 sibling of the main Onewheel service; shared 0800200c9a66 node.

evidence_array.description: Widely documented in the Onewheel reverse-engineering community as the main service UUID.

UUID_purpose: 0xFEFE is the Bluetooth SIG 16-bit UUID assigned to GN ReSound A/S (hearing-aid manufacturer; makers of ReSound, Beltone, Interton hearing aids supporting BLE ASHA streaming). The non-canonical 128-bit padding here suggests a 16-bit service UUID + service-data bytes concatenated and stored as a 128-bit string by some scanner — capture artifact rather than a true 128-bit service identifier.

evidence_array.description: 0xFEFE = GN ReSound A/S SIG assignment 2013-12-17. The non-canonical 128-bit form is a scanner concatenation artifact.

evidence_array.description: Gadgetbridge's Garmin Protocol page documents this base UUID family. OpenFenix/bluetooth-sniffer Garmin reverse-engineering project also enumerates UUIDs in this family.

UUID_purpose: Garmin watch/sensor/dive-computer BLE service; matches Garmin's well-known custom base 6a4e****-667b-11e3-949a-0800200c9a66 used by their watches for the Multi-Link (ML/MLR) and GFDI protocols. The exact 3e10 sub-identifier is not in the publicly documented Multi-Link characteristic map (28xx range) — could be a newer/older characteristic, dive-computer or sensor-specific characteristic, or a non-Multilink Garmin service.

evidence_array.description: Gadgetbridge's Garmin Protocol page documents the base 6a4e****-667b-11e3-949a-0800200c9a66 as Garmin's namespace (Multi-Link service 6a4e2800, characteristics 6a4e2810-2814 read, 6a4e2820-2824 write, 6a4e4c80, 6a4ecd28, 6a4e8022). evidence_array.description: Decodes to canonical Google Android Auto UUID 4de17a00-52cb-11e6-bdf4-0800200c9a66 (per bluez issue #963) under field-swapped Microsoft GUID byte layout interpretation.

evidence_array.description: Direct read of the Google-archived Android BluetoothChat sample's BluetoothChatService.java confirms `private static final UUID MY_UUID_INSECURE = UUID.fromString('8ce255c0-200a-11e0-ac64-0800200c9a66')`.

UUID_name: BLE Serial Port Service (vSP family, X=4 variant)

evidence_array.description: Axelle Apvrille's BSidesMunich 2020 talk and the fsalomon/nespresso-expert-ble repo both identify this UUID as COFFEE_MACHINE_SERVICE_UUID for the Nespresso machine and enumerate its five characteristics.

evidence_array.description: BLEScope ties this UUID exclusively to package 'com.nest.android'. The 0xfffd setup characteristic disclosed in the 2017 Jason Doyle Nest Cam BLE buffer-overflow advisory (Exploit-DB 41643) lives under this service.

UUID_purpose: Custom 128-bit BLE base UUID used by Nikon mirrorless and DSLR cameras (Z-series, D5600/D7500, COOLPIX A-series, etc.) for the always-on SnapBridge Bluetooth Low Energy link used for pairing, location/clock sync, and JPEG auto-transfer. 0x2000-0x200B and 0x2080-0x2081 characteristics live under the same base; even the standard 0x2A19 Battery Level is re-exposed under this base.

evidence_array.description: BLEScope shows this UUID and its sibling characteristics (00002000..0000200B-3dd4-4255-8d62-6dc7b9bd5561, plus 0x2080/0x2081 and 0x2A19) registered exclusively by packages 'com.nikon.snapbridge.cmru' and 'com.nikon.snapbridge.sb360170' — Nikon's official SnapBridge Android apps.

evidence_array.description: BLEScope shows the entire family registered exclusively by Nikon SnapBridge packages com.nikon.snapbridge.cmru and com.nikon.snapbridge.sb360170.

evidence_array.description: Multiple Windows driver databases (driverpack.io, driveridentifier.com, drivers.eu) list this BTHENUM hardware-ID string with the description 'Nokia OBEX PC Suite Services' across many host laptops. The OUI 00:02:EE belongs to Nokia per the IEEE OUI registry.

UUID_name: PKOC Legacy Service (Public Key Open Credential)

Companion characteristics: fe278a85-... = Write, e5b1b3b5-... = Read.

evidence_array.description: PSIA's official com-psia-pkoc reference repo defines ServiceLegacyUUID = '41fb60a1-d4d0-4ae9-8cbb-b62b5ae81810' alongside ServiceUUID = '0000FFF0-...'.

evidence_array.description: Gist documenting the Nanlite RF/BLE protocol identifies the UUID as the USR WH-BLE 102 module's UART service with TX/RX characteristics; the module is a generic transparent-serial bridge used by multiple downstream vendors.

UUID_purpose: Regex covering all Withings device-specific BLE service UUIDs. The fixed prefix 00000020-5749-5448 contains ASCII 'WITH' (57 49 54 48). The trailing 8 bytes encode a 2-byte device-type field and the device's MAC (usually Withings OUI 00:24:E4:xx:xx:xx).

evidence_array.description: Withings devices burn their MAC into the UUID after the 'WITH' ASCII signature. OUI 00:24:E4 is registered to Withings.

UUID_purpose: Withings BLE service template with the per-device MAC zeroed out. Same 'WITH' (57 49 54 48) ASCII signature as the MAC-bearing variants.

evidence_array.description: Same 'WITH' signature as the per-device variants; trailing MAC bytes zeroed.

UUID_purpose: Withings device-specific BLE service (variant). Bytes 4-7 spell 'WITH'; trailing 6 bytes are the device MAC 00:24:E4:6F:4D:50 with Withings OUI 00:24:E4.

evidence_array.description: ASCII 'WITH' + Withings OUI 00:24:E4 embedded.

UUID_purpose: Withings device-specific BLE service. Bytes 4-7 spell 'WITH' (Withings) in ASCII. Bytes 10-15 are the device's MAC starting with the Withings IEEE OUI 00:24:E4 (registered 2009-01-19). Used across Withings BLE products: scales, BP monitors, sleep mats, watches, thermometers.

evidence_array.description: OUI 00:24:E4 = Withings (Issy-les-Moulineaux, France). ASCII 'WITH' (57 49 54 48) signature embedded at bytes 4-7.

UUID_purpose: Withings proprietary GATT service. Bytes 5749 5448 = ASCII 'WITH' (Withings signature). Used in Body+ scales, Sleep, BPM Core, ScanWatch, Pulse, Thermo.

evidence_array.description: ASCII bytes 5749 5448 = 'WITH' (Withings signature embedded in UUID).

UUID_purpose: BLE UART service for the Wurth Elektronik Proteus-e nRF52-based module. Transparent UART-over-BLE; companion characteristics 6e400002-... (write) and 6e400003-... (notify). Note: shares the leading 6e400001 short-form with the Nordic UART Service (NUS) but uses Wurth's own base UUID (c352-11e5-953d-0002a5d5c51b).

evidence_array.description: Wurth Proteus-Connect iOS app source documents this UUID. Documented in Proteus-e quickstart guide.

UUID_purpose: Custom BLE service exposed by the Xiaomi M365 electric scooter's BLE microcontroller for companion-app communication (firmware update, lock/unlock, telemetry, riding mode).

evidence_array.description: Referenced in the ScooterHacking firmware index and community reverse-engineering work on Xiaomi M365 BLE.

UUID_purpose: Primary BLE GATT service exposed by the Yamaha YAS-108 Bluetooth-controlled soundbar. Characteristic 0c50e7fa-594c-408b-ae0d-b53b884b7c08 = write (push commands); 5cafe9de-e7b0-4e0b-8fb9-2da91a7ae3ed = notify.

evidence_array.description: masayoshikuroda/soundbar repo (Yamaha YAS-108 reverse-engineering project) defines `SoundBar.NAME = 'YAS-108_BLE'` and `SoundBar.SERVICE_UUID = '945ca2b0-852c-4ab8-b654-354df41c2795'`. Also confirmed in companion macro XML files.

UUID_purpose: BLE characteristic for the Yeelight BTO Willow Lightstrip. UUID bytes (offset 4+) ASCII-decode to 'idso@lloliW' which reverses to 'Willow@osdiy' — the project codename. Used by the Yeelight BLE remote/dimmer.

evidence_array.description: ASCII signature 'Willow' (codename of Yeelight BTO lightstrip BLE product line).

UUID_name: Unknown Zebra BLE service (likely Link-OS auxiliary)
UUID_purpose: UUIDv1 from 2016-04-22 with node 0002a5d5c51b — same fixed node used by Zebra's documented Parser Service 38eb4a80-c570-11e3-9507-0002a5d5c51b. Different timestamp, so separate Zebra BLE service.
BLE GATT service used by MCUmgr / SMP for DFU, filesystem access, statistics, OS management on Zephyr RTOS, Apache Mynewt, nRF Connect SDK.
BLE service used by the nymea-networkmanager daemon to let a client configure and monitor a wireless (WiFi) network connection over BLE. Common on nymea-based open-source IoT gateways.

evidence_array.description: nymea-networkmanager README documents this UUID as the BLE 'Wireless service' for WiFi setup.

UUID_purpose: BLE service for the open-source smarchGlasses smart-glasses project. Defined as `glassesLEDService` in the iOS controller. Also seen advertised by CXL-2404 device in `DowLucas/perfectemp` BLE protocol docs.

evidence_array.description: Defines `glassesLEDService` with this UUID in the public smarchGlasses iOS controller.

UUID_purpose: Primary service UUID for u-blox Serial Port Service over BLE on NINA-B series modules. Companion chars d703 (FIFO data), d704 (credit-based flow control).
